title: centaurea alba subsp. caliacrae
intro: Centaurea alba subsp. caliacrae, also known as a mountain cornflower, is a flowering plant species belonging to the aster family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. Centaurea alba subsp. caliacrae can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
